properties.notes: A small town up the Arno river valley (hence "Valdarno") next to a Roman road and an old church.
There is more than one option for this location, but this appears the most likely.
Also called Bell-lloc in Valencian - this is probably not the Bell-Lloc near Girona in the north.
The original is water damaged; There's also a possible Linares de Mora in Valencia.
Also written Ontinyent in Valencian.
It is possible this could be  Monreal del Campo (in Aragon rather than Navarre).  The coordinates here are in Navarre near Pamplona
This is possible, but other locations are possible. The document mentions a ship heading east from Aiguas-mortes in France and stopping in Monaco.

Uncertain location.  The letter says "Vergigno" which could be a neighborhood of Florence or the Villa de Vergigno in Montelupo Fiorentino.  Either way it is within the larger metropolitan area of Florence.
Similar to Cap Blanc, this is a quick note written on a ship near this cape near Denia in Valencia, so on their way to another location.
A village that is part of the city of Genoa, on the coast to the east.
At what is now San Piero a Grado, a suburb of Pisa. Was the seaport of of the Republic of Pisa, but was held by Genoa in the late fourteenth century.
The letter itself says they are looking at shipping wool through Peñiscola and the actual origin is "porell" with an abbreviation.
Spelled Villanueva i la Geltru in Catalan.
Also called Lizarra (Basque).
Also spelled Jativa
Also spelled Xabia, province of Alicante
South of Montpellier on the coast
Province of Savona southwest of Genoa, this is the Isola di Gallinara in Albenga.
Agde is drawn from a reading of the original document.  Agde is also included in Federigo Melis' list of cities.
The letter says "Fonte de Periglio" - the old name for Perello is Font del Perello.
Federigo Melis identifies this as the island of Rhodes though it could be one of the towns called Rodi in Puglia or Sicily.

The Letter says "in buona" and is sent to Pisa.  The second letter from the same location contains a postscript sent almost four months later from Tunis. Melis states that this is in Algeria and Bona is the old name of the city of Annaba.
A district of the city of Prato.
Given the content of the letter and the destination, the most likely Casanova is the small town north of Genoa (which is the letter's destination.)
Directly south of San Miniato, in the province of Florence. Tonda itself is a 12th century castle in the region of Montaione.
Also called Vistabella del Maestrat (Valencian)

I am assuming this is Sestri Levante, which is east along the coast from Genoa, rather than Sestri Ponente which is in the city itself. The letter only says Sestri.  Dante calls Sestri Levante only "Seistri" as well.
On the island of Mallorca at the far southern tip. Also called Cap de ses Salines.
This location is not secure.  The letter states "La Rocha" clearly, but not Corba.  It could be a mountain monastery near Girona or the town north of Andorra la Vella.
There are other possibilities for this location - less secure than other attributions here.
There are two - one right next to Prato, one west of Lucca, but the Prato location has a "Via del Palco."
South of Florence and near the Colle di Val Elsa
A cape East of Toulon - It was a 12th century castle under the control of Hyeres, but changed owners in the 13th and 14th centuries.
A district of Montelupo Fiorentino, a comune of the city of Florence.
On the coast north of Benidorm south of Valencia. Although this just means "White Cape," the letter actually notes that they are "in a ship off Cap Blanch" and headed towards Mallorca.
This location is not certain.  It is listed as part of Sant'Anna Arresi, on the southwest coast of Sardinia.
North end of the Ebro River delta
While there is a town of Scio north of Verona, a letter from Scio mentions a man returning from Alexandria from Famagusta pointing to an Eastern location.
The letters from Malines contain notes about Bruges, hence the location in Belgium.
Inland from Vinaros, very close to Castell de Cabres
This is the Catholic parish of San Martino a Gangalandi which is in the town of Signa on the Arno - an old port location.
Town in the Metropolitan City of Turin
The Genoan port district in Constantinople - a castle just north of the city.
